metadata.consolidation: 37 -> 30 tools: deleted 3 (non-atomic pipelines), merged 4 (subtypes into parent tools)

prompts.system:
You are an expert at designing tool-based pipelines for video understanding systems.
Given a question that must be answered by watching a video, identify the specific tools needed,
their execution order, and what each tool resolves in this question.
Each tool should be an atomic, reusable capability (not a full pipeline).
prompts.user_template:
## Question
{question_text}

## Task
List all tools a video understanding system needs to answer this question.
For each tool, provide:
1. name: concise English snake_case name (prefer `{{target}}_{{operation}}` form, e.g., object_detection, action_recognition)
2. description: what this tool does in general (one sentence)
3. parameters: key arguments that specify WHAT the tool operates on for THIS question (as a dict)
4. purpose_in_question: what specific information it extracts/computes for THIS question
5. step: execution order (1, 2, 3...)

Return a JSON array ordered by step:
[
  {{
    "step": 1,
    "name": "tool_name",
    "description": "General description of what this tool does",
    "parameters": {{"target": "...", ...}},
    "purpose_in_question": "What it specifically does for this question"
  }},
  ...
]

Guidelines:
- Each tool must be ATOMIC — it does exactly ONE thing. Do NOT combine perception + reasoning or detection + tracking in one tool.
- Use GENERIC names, not scene-specific names.
  GOOD: object_detection, ocr, action_recognition
  BAD: door_detector, serve_speed_extraction, scoreboard_ocr (these are just object_detection / ocr / ocr applied to specific targets — put the specific target in "parameters" instead)
- Include both perception tools (extracting info from video) and reasoning tools (computing/comparing results).
- A typical question needs 3-8 tools.
- Use English snake_case for names.
- Only output the JSON array, no other text.
prompts.reconcile:
You manage a tool registry for a video understanding system. Each tool is ONE reusable atomic capability. A tool is defined by its OPERATION TYPE — different targets/domains go in parameters, not separate tools.

## Current Registry ({n_tools} tools)
{registry_json}

## Raw Proposals (ability: {ability_name})
{proposals_json}

## Core Principle
Before deciding ADD, always ask: "Could an existing tool do this with different parameter values?" If yes → MAP.
- Same operation, different target → MAP. E.g. "door_detector", "ball_detection" → object_detection; "score_ocr", "overlay_reading" → text_recognition; "emotion_recognition", "light_state" → attribute_recognition.
- Same modality, different granularity → MAP. E.g. "segment_retrieval", "temporal_localization", "scene_finding" → temporal_grounding; "frame_extraction", "frame_sampling" → frame_extraction.
- Pure logic/reasoning without perception → SKIP (do not add). E.g. "answer_selection", "event_ordering", "arithmetic".

## ADD — only when genuinely no existing tool covers this operation type
- Name: `{{target}}_{{operation}}`, 2 words, snake_case, generic
- Atomic: one perception/analysis operation, not a pipeline
- Definition ≤100 tokens, specific I/O types (not bare "dict")

## Output JSON
{{"decisions": [
  {{"raw_name": "xxx", "action": "MAP", "registry_name": "existing_tool"}},
  {{"raw_name": "yyy", "action": "ADD", "tool": {{"name": "tool_name", "description": "...", "input": {{"k": "type"}}, "output": {{"k": "type"}}}}}},
  {{"raw_name": "zzz", "action": "SKIP"}}
]}}

abilities.ability_description: 算术和数字逻辑，如计算、百分比、速度、时间差等
对离散事件的量化汇总：数物体、数动作、数事件次数
先后顺序与因果推理，如第几个、排序、因果链
利用视觉和空间线索推断场景宏观意义，如推断原因、意图、隐含信息
多维信息汇总，如结合语音内容、文字信息和画面来综合推理
在什么时候发生，根据描述找到对应的时间片段或检测起止时间
事件发生顺序，理解前后因果和逻辑链条
物体从状态A到状态B的变化，或视频镜头的切换点
追踪过程的持续性：物体轨迹、动作时长估算、持续状态
识别和定义持续动作或时间段，如识别正在进行的活动
物体在空间中的坐标、边界或存在位置
物体间的相对位置关系：上下左右、里外、朝向、遮挡
在空间维度上捕捉目标的移动路径和位移规律
根据位置变化推断物体状态，或对复杂空间布局进行逻辑判断
说了什么：语音转文字、对话内容识别
声画同步/谁在说话，将视觉画面与音频信号对齐或语义关联
非语音类环境音识别，如背景音乐、物体碰撞声等
通用物体感知：是什么物体、在哪里、有多少
文本提取与语义理解：读取画面中的文字、标题、字幕等
在干什么、发生了什么事件
颜色、状态、精细属性、场景分类等
人体结构与生物特征识别：身份、姿态、视线、表情等

tools.name: text_recognition
tools.description: Extracts and recognizes text content from video frames, including overlays, signs, scoreboards, and captions.

output.texts: list[{text: str, frame_index: int, bounding_box: list[float], confidence: float}]

tools.name: temporal_grounding
tools.description: Locates temporal segments in a video that match a natural language query or specified visual/audio conditions. Returns start and end timestamps.

output.segments: list[{start_time: float, end_time: float, confidence: float}]

tools.name: action_recognition
tools.description: Detects and classifies human actions, gestures, or motion events within a video segment. Covers full-body actions (walking, jumping), communicative gestures (pointing, waving), and motion phase segmentation (direction shifts, pauses). Returns identified actions with temporal boundaries.

output.actions: list[{action: str, start_time: float, end_time: float, bounding_box: list[float]|null, phase: str|null, confidence: float}]

tools.name: audio_event_detection
tools.description: Detects and classifies salient sound events in the audio track, including environmental sounds, music, and speech events. For music segments, can identify song title and artist via acoustic fingerprinting.

output.events: list[{sound: str, start_time: float, end_time: float, confidence: float, music_info: {title: str, artist: str}|null}]

tools.name: camera_motion_analysis
tools.description: Analyzes camera behavior including pan, tilt, zoom, and focus shifts over time. Identifies what the camera is focusing on or tracking.

output.camera_events: list[{type: str, start_time: float, end_time: float, focus_target: str|null, confidence: float}]

tools.name: object_detection
tools.description: Detects and localizes objects of interest in video frames, returning bounding boxes and class labels.

output.detections: list[{object: str, frame_index: int, bounding_box: list[float], confidence: float}]

tools.name: frame_extraction
tools.description: Samples or extracts relevant frames or short clips from a video based on temporal criteria or content-based query.

tools.name: object_tracking
tools.description: Tracks a specified object across consecutive video frames, returning its trajectory and positional data over time.

output.trajectory: list[{frame_index: int, bounding_box: list[float], timestamp: float}]

tools.name: motion_speed_estimation
tools.description: Estimates the speed and velocity of a tracked object based on its positional changes across frames and frame rate metadata.

output.speed_estimates: list[{timestamp: float, speed: float, direction: str|null}]

tools.name: spatial_analysis
tools.description: Analyzes spatial relationships between detected objects in a frame, including relative positions, distances, and arrangements (e.g., left of, between, above, nearest).

output.relationships: list[{subject: str, relation: str, reference: str, distance: float|null, confidence: float}]

tools.name: attribute_recognition
tools.description: Detects and classifies visual attributes or properties of objects in video frames, such as color, state, illumination, material, or attached indicators.

output.attributes: list[{frame_index: int, object_region: list[float], attribute: str, value: str, confidence: float}]

tools.name: object_counting
tools.description: Counts the number of instances of a specified object, action, or event within video frames or a temporal segment. Supports both spatial counting in frames and temporal repetition counting.

input.detections: list[{object: str, bounding_box: list[float], frame_index: int}]|null

tools.name: person_identification
tools.description: Identifies and recognizes specific individuals in video frames based on facial features, body appearance, or other distinguishing characteristics. Returns identity labels with bounding boxes.

output.identifications: list[{identity: str, frame_index: int, bounding_box: list[float], confidence: float}]

tools.name: speech_transcription
tools.description: Transcribes spoken language from the audio track of a video into timestamped text. Returns recognized words and phrases with temporal boundaries.

output.transcriptions: list[{text: str, start_time: float, end_time: float, confidence: float}]

tools.name: scene_recognition
tools.description: Identifies and classifies the scene, location, or setting depicted in video frames, returning scene labels such as indoor/outdoor, room type, or environment category.

output.scenes: list[{frame_index: int, scene: str, confidence: float}]

tools.name: gaze_estimation
tools.description: Estimates the gaze or head orientation direction of detected persons in video frames, returning gaze vectors or angular directions relative to the camera.

output.gaze_estimates: list[{frame_index: int, bounding_box: list[float], yaw: float, pitch: float, gaze_vector: list[float], confidence: float}]

tools.name: instance_segmentation
tools.description: Detects and segments individual object instances in video frames, producing per-pixel masks and class labels for each instance. Enables precise separation of overlapping or adjacent objects.

output.instances: list[{object: str, frame_index: int, bounding_box: list[float], mask: image, instance_id: int, confidence: float}]

tools.name: shot_boundary_detection
tools.description: Detects visual discontinuities (cuts, fades, transitions) in a video to segment it into distinct shots or scenes. Returns boundary timestamps and transition types.

output.boundaries: list[{timestamp: float, transition_type: str, confidence: float}]

tools.name: interaction_recognition
tools.description: Detects and classifies interaction events between entities in a video segment, including the semantic roles of each participant (agent, recipient, instrument). Returns interaction type, participants with roles, and temporal boundaries.

input.entity_tracks: list[{entity: str, trajectory: list[{bounding_box: list[float], timestamp: float}]}]|null

output.interactions: list[{interaction: str, participants: list[{entity: str, role: str}], start_time: float, end_time: float, confidence: float}]

tools.name: emotion_analysis
tools.description: Analyzes the emotional tone, mood, and affective qualities conveyed by visual and audio elements in a video segment. Returns detected emotions with temporal boundaries and confidence scores.

output.emotions: list[{emotion: str, valence: float, arousal: float, start_time: float, end_time: float, modality: str, confidence: float}]

tools.name: speaker_diarization
tools.description: Segments audio by speaker identity and labels distinct speakers across the audio track. Returns speaker labels with temporal boundaries, enabling attribution of speech segments to individual speakers.

output.speaker_segments: list[{speaker_id: str, start_time: float, end_time: float, confidence: float}]

tools.name: pose_estimation
tools.description: Estimates body joint and keypoint positions for detected persons in video frames, returning skeletal pose data including limb positions, joint angles, and body posture classification.

output.poses: list[{frame_index: int, bounding_box: list[float], keypoints: list[{name: str, x: float, y: float, confidence: float}], posture: str|null, confidence: float}]

tools.name: frame_comparison
tools.description: Compares visual content between two or more video frames to identify and characterize differences, changes, or state transitions in objects, scenes, or regions. Returns detected changes with locations and descriptions.

output.changes: list[{description: str, region: list[float]|null, frame_indices: list[int], change_type: str, confidence: float}]

tools.name: video_metadata
tools.description: Extracts file-level metadata from a video, including total duration, frame rate, resolution, and codec information.

tools.name: layout_analysis
tools.description: Analyzes the structural layout composition of video frames, detecting split screens, picture-in-picture regions, overlay panels, video call grids, and other spatial partitioning of the visual canvas. Returns identified layout regions with their types and bounding boxes.

output.regions: list[{frame_index: int, layout_type: str, bounding_box: list[float], confidence: float}]

tools.name: spatial_crop
tools.description: Crops a specified spatial region from video frames, producing new frames containing only the content within the given bounding box. Used to isolate sub-regions for downstream analysis.

tools.name: video_segment_extraction
tools.description: Extracts a continuous temporal sub-clip from a video given start and end timestamps, preserving both visual and audio tracks. Returns a playable video segment for downstream analysis.

tools.name: object_reidentification
tools.description: Matches and re-identifies a specific object instance across non-consecutive video segments or shots based on visual appearance features. Returns matched occurrences with locations and similarity scores.

output.matches: list[{frame_index: int, bounding_box: list[float], similarity: float, confidence: float}]

tools.name: audio_extraction
tools.description: Separates and extracts the audio track from a video or video segment, producing a standalone audio stream for downstream analysis such as speech transcription or sound event detection.

tools.name: active_speaker_detection
tools.description: Detects which visible person is actively speaking at each moment by jointly analyzing facial/mouth movements in video frames and corresponding audio. Returns speaker regions with temporal boundaries indicating active speech intervals.

output.active_speakers: list[{bounding_box: list[float], start_time: float, end_time: float, speaker_id: str|null, confidence: float}]

questions.question_text:
Why does the lens focus on a number when the set score is 0-1, game score is 1-1, point score is 15-15?
(A) The serve speed is really high
(B) The serve speed is really low
(C) The ball hit this numbe

gemini-3.1-pro-preview.name: ocr
gemini-3.1-pro-preview.description: Extracts text from video frames.
gemini-3.1-pro-preview.purpose_in_question: Locate the timeframe where the set score is 0-1, game score is 1-1, and point score is 15-15.

gemini-3.1-pro-preview.name: scene_retrieval
gemini-3.1-pro-preview.description: Finds video segments matching a specific visual description.

parameters.query: camera focusing on a number display

gemini-3.1-pro-preview.purpose_in_question: Find the exact moment the lens focuses on a number display during the identified timeframe.

gemini-3.1-pro-preview.name: ocr
gemini-3.1-pro-preview.description: Extracts text from video frames.
gemini-3.1-pro-preview.purpose_in_question: Read the value of the number the camera is focusing on to determine the serve speed.

gemini-3.1-pro-preview.name: action_recognition
gemini-3.1-pro-preview.description: Detects specific actions or events in a video segment.

parameters.target: tennis ball hitting the number display

gemini-3.1-pro-preview.purpose_in_question: Check if the ball hit the number display to evaluate option C.

gemini-3.1-pro-preview.name: audio_analysis
gemini-3.1-pro-preview.description: Analyzes the audio track to detect specific sounds or events.
gemini-3.1-pro-preview.purpose_in_question: Check if someone is shouting around the time the number is shown to evaluate option D.

gemini-3.1-pro-preview.name: logic_reasoning
gemini-3.1-pro-preview.description: Synthesizes extracted information to answer a multiple-choice question.

options: The serve speed is really high
The serve speed is really low
Someone is shouting around the number

gemini-3.1-pro-preview.purpose_in_question: Compare the extracted serve speed value against typical speeds and use the presence/absence of other events to select the correct reason for the camera focus.

claude-opus-4-6.name: ocr
claude-opus-4-6.description: Extracts text and numbers from specified regions of video frames
claude-opus-4-6.purpose_in_question: Locate the specific moment when set score is 0-1, game score is 1-1, and point score is 15-15

claude-opus-4-6.name: temporal_localization
claude-opus-4-6.description: Identifies the specific time segment in the video matching given conditions
claude-opus-4-6.purpose_in_question: Narrow down the exact frames/segment where the specified score state occurs

claude-opus-4-6.name: camera_focus_detection
claude-opus-4-6.description: Detects what the camera lens is focusing on or zooming into in a given segment
claude-opus-4-6.purpose_in_question: Identify the specific number that the lens focuses on during the matched score state

claude-opus-4-6.name: ocr
claude-opus-4-6.description: Extracts text and numbers from specified regions of video frames
claude-opus-4-6.purpose_in_question: Read the actual number that the camera is focusing on (e.g., serve speed display)

claude-opus-4-6.name: object_detection
claude-opus-4-6.description: Detects and localizes objects of interest in video frames
claude-opus-4-6.purpose_in_question: Determine if the focused number is a serve speed display and detect ball trajectory relative to the number

claude-opus-4-6.name: action_recognition
claude-opus-4-6.description: Recognizes and classifies actions or events occurring in video segments
claude-opus-4-6.purpose_in_question: Identify if a serve just occurred and characterize the serve event context

claude-opus-4-6.name: event_context_analysis
claude-opus-4-6.description: Analyzes the contextual relationship between detected events and camera behavior
claude-opus-4-6.purpose_in_question: Determine why the camera focused on the number — whether due to high/low serve speed, ball hitting the number, or someone shouting

claude-opus-4-6.name: answer_reasoning
claude-opus-4-6.description: Synthesizes all extracted information to select the correct answer from multiple choices

parameters.evidence: all_previous_steps

claude-opus-4-6.purpose_in_question: Choose the correct reason why the lens focuses on the number given all contextual evidence

gpt-5.4.name: frame_sampling
gpt-5.4.description: Selects relevant frames or short clips from the video for downstream analysis.

parameters.focus: moments where the scoreboard shows set 0-1, game 1-1, point 15-15 and the camera/lens focuses on a number

gpt-5.4.purpose_in_question: Find the time segment where the specified score state appears and the lens zooms or cuts to the number in question.

gpt-5.4.name: ocr
gpt-5.4.description: Reads visible text and numbers from image regions.

parameters.target: on-screen scoreboard and zoomed-in number displays
text_fields: numeric value shown when lens focuses

gpt-5.4.purpose_in_question: Verify that the score is 0-1, 1-1, 15-15 and extract the number the lens focuses on.

gpt-5.4.name: camera_motion_analysis
gpt-5.4.description: Determines camera behavior such as pan, zoom, and focus shifts over time.

parameters.target: video frames around the identified score state

gpt-5.4.purpose_in_question: Confirm that the broadcast intentionally shifts attention to the number rather than the court action.

gpt-5.4.name: audio_event_detection
gpt-5.4.description: Detects salient sound events in the audio track.

parameters.target: audio aligned with the close-up on the number

gpt-5.4.purpose_in_question: Determine whether there is shouting or notable audio activity around the time the lens focuses on the number.

gpt-5.4.name: ball_detection
gpt-5.4.description: Locates the ball in individual frames.

parameters.region: court and area containing the highlighted number during the relevant play

gpt-5.4.purpose_in_question: Check whether the ball is visually present near or contacting the number.

gpt-5.4.name: object_tracking
gpt-5.4.description: Tracks a detected object across consecutive frames.

parameters.time_window: from serve initiation through the close-up on the number

gpt-5.4.purpose_in_question: Follow the ball trajectory to see whether it travels into and hits the number.

gpt-5.4.name: motion_speed_estimation
gpt-5.4.description: Estimates an object's speed from its motion across frames.

parameters.time_window: serve sequence immediately before the lens focuses on the number

gpt-5.4.purpose_in_question: Estimate whether the serve speed is unusually high or low as candidate explanations.

gpt-5.4.name: causal_option_ranking
gpt-5.4.description: Compares extracted evidence against multiple answer choices and ranks the best-supported explanation.

options: The serve speed is really high
The serve speed is really low
Someone is shouting around the number

gpt-5.4.purpose_in_question: Use the gathered visual and audio evidence to decide which answer choice best explains why the lens focuses on the number.

questions.question_text:
How many doors have colored lights at 00:29?
(A) 2
(B) 1
(C) 3
(D) 0

gemini-3.1-pro-preview.name: frame_extraction
gemini-3.1-pro-preview.description: Extracts a specific frame from the video based on a given timestamp.
gemini-3.1-pro-preview.purpose_in_question: Extract the exact video frame at 00:29 to analyze the scene.

gemini-3.1-pro-preview.name: object_detection
gemini-3.1-pro-preview.description: Detects and bounds specific objects within an image or frame.
gemini-3.1-pro-preview.purpose_in_question: Identify and locate all doors in the extracted frame.

gemini-3.1-pro-preview.name: object_detection
gemini-3.1-pro-preview.description: Detects and bounds specific objects within an image or frame.
gemini-3.1-pro-preview.purpose_in_question: Identify and locate all colored lights in the extracted frame.

gemini-3.1-pro-preview.name: spatial_relationship_analysis
gemini-3.1-pro-preview.description: Analyzes the spatial relationship between two sets of detected objects.
gemini-3.1-pro-preview.purpose_in_question: Determine which of the detected doors have colored lights on them.

gemini-3.1-pro-preview.name: counting
gemini-3.1-pro-preview.description: Counts the number of items in a given set.
gemini-3.1-pro-preview.purpose_in_question: Count the final number of doors that have colored lights to answer the question.

claude-opus-4-6.name: frame_extraction
claude-opus-4-6.description: Extracts a specific frame from a video at a given timestamp
claude-opus-4-6.purpose_in_question: Extract the frame at 00:29 to analyze the scene for doors with colored lights

claude-opus-4-6.name: object_detection
claude-opus-4-6.description: Detects and localizes specific objects within an image
claude-opus-4-6.purpose_in_question: Identify and locate all doors visible in the extracted frame at 00:29

claude-opus-4-6.name: light_detection
claude-opus-4-6.description: Detects the presence and color of lights or illumination on or near objects
claude-opus-4-6.purpose_in_question: Determine which of the detected doors have colored lights on or around them

claude-opus-4-6.name: object_counting
claude-opus-4-6.description: Counts the number of objects matching specified criteria
claude-opus-4-6.purpose_in_question: Count the total number of doors that have colored lights

claude-opus-4-6.name: answer_selection
claude-opus-4-6.description: Selects the correct answer from multiple choice options based on computed results

parameters.input: count of doors with colored lights

claude-opus-4-6.purpose_in_question: Match the counted number of doors with colored lights to the correct multiple choice answer

gpt-5.4.name: timestamp_frame_extraction
gpt-5.4.description: Extracts the video frame corresponding to a specified timestamp.
gpt-5.4.purpose_in_question: Get the exact frame at 00:29 where the doors and their lights must be inspected.

gpt-5.4.name: object_detection
gpt-5.4.description: Detects instances of specified object categories in an image or video frame.
gpt-5.4.purpose_in_question: Locate all visible doors in the frame so each door can be checked for a colored light.

gpt-5.4.name: attribute_detection
gpt-5.4.description: Determines whether specified visual attributes or attached indicators are present on detected objects.
gpt-5.4.purpose_in_question: Identify which detected doors have a colored light on or near them at 00:29.

gpt-5.4.name: counting
gpt-5.4.description: Counts the number of items in a set that satisfy a given condition.
gpt-5.4.purpose_in_question: Compute how many doors have colored lights at 00:29.

gpt-5.4.name: option_matching
gpt-5.4.description: Matches a computed result to the corresponding multiple-choice answer option.
gpt-5.4.purpose_in_question: Map the counted number of doors with colored lights to the correct answer choice.

questions.question_text:
What is the time difference in seconds between the second and third place in the competition results shown at the end of the video?
A. 0.19s.
B. 0.06s.
C. 0.25s.
D. 1.06s.

gemini-3.1-pro-preview.name: scene_retrieval
gemini-3.1-pro-preview.description: Finds video segments matching a specific visual or semantic query.

parameters.query: competition results scoreboard or ranking screen

gemini-3.1-pro-preview.purpose_in_question: Locate the segment at the end of the video that displays the final competition results.

gemini-3.1-pro-preview.name: ocr
gemini-3.1-pro-preview.description: Extracts text from images or video frames.
gemini-3.1-pro-preview.purpose_in_question: Read all the text on the competition results screen to capture the rankings and their corresponding times.

gemini-3.1-pro-preview.name: information_extraction
gemini-3.1-pro-preview.description: Extracts specific structured information from unstructured text.
gemini-3.1-pro-preview.purpose_in_question: Parse the extracted OCR text to identify and isolate the specific time values for the 2nd and 3rd place finishers.

gemini-3.1-pro-preview.name: math_calculator
gemini-3.1-pro-preview.description: Performs mathematical calculations on given numbers.
gemini-3.1-pro-preview.purpose_in_question: Calculate the difference in seconds between the third place time and second place time to determine the final answer.

claude-opus-4-6.name: video_segment_detection
claude-opus-4-6.description: Identifies and locates specific segments or scenes within a video based on temporal position or content type
claude-opus-4-6.purpose_in_question: Locate the results/standings screen shown at the end of the video

claude-opus-4-6.name: frame_extraction
claude-opus-4-6.description: Extracts specific frames from a video segment for detailed analysis
claude-opus-4-6.purpose_in_question: Extract a clear frame of the competition results for OCR processing

claude-opus-4-6.name: ocr
claude-opus-4-6.description: Extracts text and numerical data from an image or video frame

parameters.target: competition results table including place rankings and times

claude-opus-4-6.purpose_in_question: Read the times associated with second place and third place finishers from the results display

claude-opus-4-6.name: text_parsing
claude-opus-4-6.description: Parses and structures extracted text into meaningful data fields
claude-opus-4-6.purpose_in_question: Identify and isolate the specific time values for the 2nd place and 3rd place competitors

claude-opus-4-6.name: arithmetic_computation
claude-opus-4-6.description: Performs mathematical calculations on numerical values
claude-opus-4-6.purpose_in_question: Calculate the time difference in seconds between the second and third place finishers

claude-opus-4-6.name: multiple_choice_matching
claude-opus-4-6.description: Matches a computed result to the closest option in a set of multiple choice answers

visual_question_answering.reason: High-level reasoning pipeline, not an atomic perception capability

video_captioning.reason: High-level generation pipeline, not an atomic perception capability

temporal_alignment.reason: Post-processing logic (timestamp matching), not a perception operation

gesture_recognition.reason: Gestures are a subtype of actions, distinguished via target_actions parameter

motion_segmentation.reason: Motion phase segmentation is temporal action boundary detection, covered by action_recognition with phase output

role_recognition.reason: Semantic roles are an output dimension of interaction recognition, not a separate operation

song_identification.reason: Song identification is a special case of audio event detection with identify_music=true
